paper_title: Chlorophyll Fluorescence Measurements in T. weissflogii

methods.description: This method describes the process of measuring chlorophyll fluorescence metrics from time induction curves and rapid light curves for T. weissflogii cells using a Xe-PAM device. It is used to assess the photosynthetic performance under different light conditions.

steps.description: Chlorophyll fluorescence metrics from time induction curves under a fixed light or from rapid light curves were measured for T. weissflogii cells using a Xe-PAM (Walz, Germany).
steps.searchable_summary: Measure chlorophyll fluorescence metrics using Xe-PAM

steps.description: For the measurements of fluorescence induction curves, the saturation light pulse was set at 5000 μmol m −2 s −1 for 0.8 s, and the LL-grown cells were measured at actinic light of 76 and 226 μmol m −2 s −1 , and for the HL-grown cells it was measured at actinic light of 226 μmol m −2 s −1.
steps.searchable_summary: Set saturation light pulse and measure fluorescence induction curves

steps.description: The rapid light curves (RPLs) were measured at actinic lights of 0, 226, 337, 533, 781, 1077, 1593, 2130 and 2854 μmol m −2 s −1 and then treated with a saturation light pulse of 5000 μmol m −2 s −1 for 0.8 s after a 10 s exposure under each actinic light level.
steps.searchable_summary: Measure rapid light curves and apply saturation light pulse

steps.description: The F V /F M , Φ PSII and NPQ under different culture conditions were obtained from the induction curves, and the calculation of parameters followed published equations (Bilger and Björkman 1990; Genty et al. 1989; Kitajima and Butler 1975).
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate F V /F M , Φ PSII, and NPQ from induction curves

steps.description: The maximum relative electron transport rate (rETR max ), light use efficiency (α) and saturation light intensity (E k ) were determined from RLC fit following Webb et al. (1974).
steps.searchable_summary: Determine rETR max, α, and E k from RLC fit
